title: 3 Top Dividend Stocks Yielding 5% or More to Buy Right Now to Boost Your Passive Income
author: The Motley Fool
description: The article highlights three high-yielding dividend stocks - Four Corners Property Trust, VICI Properties, and NNN REIT - that can provide a boost to passive income. These REITs focus on owning properties secured by long-term net leases, generating stable rental income to support attractive dividends.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: The article highlights the company's stable rental income from long-term net leases, its diversified portfolio, and its history of dividend growth, making it a good option for boosting passive income.

The article praises VICI Properties' focus on experiential real estate, its long-term net leases with inflation-linked rents, and its consistent dividend growth, making it an attractive high-yield dividend stock.
The article highlights NNN REIT's conservative investment strategy, its long-term net leases, and its impressive 35-year streak of dividend increases, making it a reliable high-yield dividend stock.

title: Here's Why You Should Keep American Tower in Your Portfolio
author: Zacks
description: American Tower's extensive and geographically diversified communication real estate portfolio positions it well to ride the growth curve amid rising capital spending by wireless carriers on the incremental demand from global 4G and 5G deployment efforts. However, customer concentration poses a key concern for the company.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: The article highlights American Tower's strong performance, expansionary efforts, and disciplined capital-allocation strategy, which position the company well to benefit from the growth in the wireless industry.
The article mentions Four Corners Property Trust as a better-ranked stock in the REIT sector, but does not provide any specific insights about the company.
The article mentions Lamar Advertising as a better-ranked stock in the REIT sector, but does not provide any specific insights about the company.

title: These Top Dividend Stocks Can Help Satisfy Your Craving for More Passive Income
author: Matt Dilallo
description: The article highlights three companies - Coca-Cola, Hormel Foods, and Four Corners Property Trust - that offer attractive dividend yields and have a strong track record of increasing their payouts over the years, making them appealing options for investors seeking passive income.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Coca-Cola offers a dividend yield of around 3%, which is roughly double the S&P 500 average. The company has an impressive record of 62 consecutive years of dividend increases, making it a Dividend King. It is expected to continue growing its revenues and earnings, supporting its ability to maintain and grow the dividend.

Hormel Foods currently yields 3.5% and has a remarkable history of 96 years of uninterrupted dividend payments, with 58 consecutive years of dividend increases. The company has a strong financial position, generating ample cash flow to cover the dividend and invest in growth, suggesting its ability to continue providing a satisfying and steadily rising dividend.

Four Corners Property Trust, a real estate investment trust (REIT), currently yields 4.5%. The company has steadily expanded its portfolio and increased its dividend every year since 2017, including a 1.5% increase last year. Its diversified tenant base and focus on income-producing properties suggest its ability to continue providing an attractive and growing dividend.

title: Places You Routinely Visit Could Provide You With a Pathway Toward a Lifetime of Passive Income
author: The Motley Fool
description: The article discusses how investing in real estate investment trusts (REITs) that own properties you frequently visit, such as grocery stores, restaurants, and auto service centers, can provide a pathway to passive income. The REITs mentioned are Agree Realty, Getty Realty, and Four Corners Property Trust.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: The article highlights Agree Realty's diversified portfolio of over 2,200 retail properties, including grocery stores, home improvement centers, and tire and auto service locations. It notes that the REIT's leases provide stable rental income, and it has a history of growing its dividend at a 5.7% annual rate over the last decade.

The article highlights Agree Realty's diversified portfolio of over 2,200 retail properties, including grocery stores, home improvement centers, and tire and auto service locations. It notes that the REIT's leases provide stable rental income, and it has a history of growing its dividend at a 5.7% annual rate over the last decade.

The article describes Getty Realty as a REIT focused on investing in convenience and retail real estate, such as convenience stores, car washes, auto service centers, and gas stations. It notes that the REIT pays out a dividend yield of over 5.5% and has grown its dividend at a 5.2% compound annual rate since 2019.

The article highlights Four Corners Property Trust's portfolio of over 1,150 properties net leased to over 150 brands, including restaurants, auto service properties, and medical retail locations. It notes that the REIT pays out around 80% of its cash flow in dividends, with a current yield of nearly 5%, and has a history of increasing its dividend.

title: Four Corners (FCPT) Acquires MercyOne Outpatient Clinic for $3M
author: Zacks Equity Research
description: To boost its portfolio with real estate leased to strong credit operators, Four Corners (FCPT) announces another acquisition of MercyOne outpatient clinic property, shelling out $3 million.
